control_card.C: One stdlib Python script writes normalized JSON, CSV, Markdown, and manifest outputs.
control_card.F: No theorem, Clay promotion, spectral gap, or continuum transfer is asserted here.
control_card.G: Promotion stays blocked until finite YM equation, Hamiltonian, spectral gap, uniform lower bound, and continuum transfer are closed.
control_card.L: BT metric selected so star is covariantly constant on admissible fields implies [d_A,*]F_A vanishes; outside that lane the commutator is leakage.
control_card.O: Worker 4 owns the Sprint166 YM/BT finite Hodge gauge-commutator chain emitter.
control_card.P: Use the artifact as a fail-closed compatibility chain and obligation ledger.
control_card.R: Normalize the finite Hodge variation chain and isolate [d_A,*]F_A as the compatibility defect/leakage term before any YM promotion.
control_card.S: Finite Hodge/IBP gives a clean variational surface; Yang-Mills needs gauge-Hodge compatibility, not just Maxwell-style d dagger equals star-d-star.

remaining_path: Close the finite YM equation, construct the finite Hamiltonian, prove a spectral gap, prove a uniform lower bound across the finite BT family, and transfer the lower bound to the continuum.

rows.bt_metric_condition: Choose a positive finite BT metric and star on the selected admissible complex.
rows.claim: A finite cell/cochain Hodge star and inner product make variation of the curvature energy meaningful after finite integration by parts.
rows.remaining_path: Prove the selected star is compatible with gauge transport on admissible fields.
rows.vanishing_condition: No vanishing claim at this stage.

rows.bt_metric_condition: Boundary pairings and cell weights must match the BT metric selection.
rows.claim: Finite IBP identifies the adjoint surface d dagger through the chosen finite star and boundary convention.
rows.defect_role: boundary and Hodge-choice leakage is visible before gauge covariance is imposed
rows.maxwell_special_case: For abelian Maxwell, after finite Hodge/IBP, d dagger = +/- * d * up to the chosen degree/sign convention.
rows.remaining_path: Separate ordinary finite adjointness from gauge-covariant adjointness.
rows.vanishing_condition: Boundary leakage vanishes only under the selected closed/admissible boundary lane.

rows.bt_metric_condition: The BT metric must be selected so the discrete star is parallel with respect to admissible gauge transport on the curvature support.
rows.claim: For nonabelian YM, the finite variational chain contains the extra compatibility defect [d_A,*]F_A; it measures failure of the Hodge star to commute with covariant differentiation on curvature.
rows.defect_role: leakage term in the attempted finite YM Euler-Lagrange reduction
rows.maxwell_special_case: In abelian Maxwell, d_A=d and the clean d dagger = +/- * d * route has no nonabelian commutator leakage.
rows.remaining_path: Prove the commutator estimate or exact vanishing in the finite BT model.
rows.vanishing_condition: If d_A F_A = 0 and d_A(*F_A) = *(d_A F_A) on admissible fields, then [d_A,*]F_A = 0.

rows.bt_metric_condition: Metric weights are gauge-invariant on stabilizers, the star maps admissible curvature cochains to admissible dual cochains, and parallel transport preserves the star pairing.
rows.claim: A selected BT metric can make the defect vanish for admissible fields when cell weights, orientations, gauge parallel transport, and boundary conditions make star covariantly constant on F_A.
rows.defect_role: zero only inside the selected admissible compatibility lane
rows.remaining_path: Formalize admissibility and prove the BT metric compatibility lemma without hidden continuum assumptions.
rows.vanishing_condition: For admissible A and F_A satisfying finite Bianchi d_A F_A = 0, covariant-star compatibility gives [d_A,*]F_A = 0.

rows.bt_metric_condition: The selected BT metric must support a closed adjoint and admissible variation class.
rows.claim: Once the defect vanishes or is absorbed, the finite YM equation must still be derived as the exact finite Euler-Lagrange equation.
rows.defect_role: unclosed leakage blocks promotion from variational surface to finite YM equation
rows.remaining_path: Close finite YM equation, Hamiltonian construction, spectral gap, uniform lower bound, and continuum transfer.
rows.vanishing_condition: Not promoted; conditional vanishing is only an input obligation.

rows.bt_metric_condition: Uniformity in the finite BT metric family must survive refinement and gauge quotienting.
rows.claim: The compatibility chain is upstream of the Clay-scale route; it does not by itself construct the Hamiltonian, prove a spectral gap, supply a uniform lower bound, or transfer to the continuum.
rows.defect_role: resolved defect is necessary but not sufficient for downstream gap transfer
rows.remaining_path: Build the finite Hamiltonian, prove positivity and nonzero uniform lower bound, establish spectral convergence, then transfer to the continuum YM measure/theory.

selected_bt_metric_defect_vanishes_when: The finite BT metric/star is covariantly constant on admissible curvature cochains, preserves boundary/admissibility conditions, and the finite Bianchi identity d_A F_A = 0 is available.
